what_it_does: This file is a sent noticeboard message from tim to claude_code that records a spoken directive to write a fork section about agent-driven research and to add background and principles without writing specific plans.
observations: Message type is 'message' with state 'sent' in channel 'dragnet-development'.
Author session is 'tim' and source is 'claude_code'.
The title is truncated mid-sentence and matches the start of the body.
Body asks for a 'whole section for the fork' covering agents that research the company, code, online sources, interface design, Claude code, and channels.
Explicitly asks for more background and principles, not proposals, specifics, or a plan.
References unresolved problems to be handled through tim's 'principles in Architecture', which are not present in the file.
Warns that the only bad thing is writing plans that influence other agents.
Created and updated timestamps are identical.
standouts: Explicit anti-plan instruction: do not write proposals, specifics, or plans.
Scope spans agents researching company internals, code, online material, interface design, and Claude Code channels.
Acknowledges missing knowledge of the author's Architecture principles and frames that as acceptable.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, created/updated, and history.
Body is free-form markdown after the frontmatter delimiter.
Title appears to be auto-truncated from the body text.
concerns: Title is cut off mid-sentence, making it read as incomplete.
Body is a single stream-of-consciousness paragraph with ambiguous scope and terminology.
Refers to external 'principles in Architecture' that are not included, so the constraint may be hard to act on.
The instruction to avoid plans may conflict with the directive to 'ride a whole section'.
notes: A rambling human directive telling an agent to add fork documentation about agent research, but strictly forbidding concrete plans or specifics.
questions: What are tim's specific 'principles in Architecture'?
Which existing section is this meant to extend?
What does 'ride a whole section' mean in this workflow?
What deliverable format is expected for the background/principles?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a directive for a documentation section; the actual content is a vague, spoken-style instruction with a strong meta-constraint, which mostly matches but is hard to execute without external context.
apparent_themes: documentation by principles rather than plans
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to shape how a fork section is written so that it provides context and guiding constraints for other agents without locking them into a particular implementation.
novelty: The message explicitly instructs the recipient not to produce plans or specifics, treating the file itself as a boundary condition for other agents rather than a design document.
contribution: Preserves a human intent and a hard constraint (principles-only, no prescriptive plans) that should govern any subsequent fork documentation generated from this channel memory.
summary_for_embedding: A sent noticeboard item in dragnet-development where tim instructs claude_code to write a fork section on agent research capabilities and to add only background and principles, avoiding plans or specifics.
intention_for_embedding: To record a principles-only directive for fork documentation about agent research while preventing the creation of prescriptive plans that could influence other agents.
